(3®nfojn) 5i. 31. vh. aw< pfvs [faW,-UWV/3IWW0|/98/07 3 DENTAL COUNCIL OF INDIA NOOTICATION New Delhi, the 27th June, 2007 No.DCI-134-2007.—In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 8(l)(d) of the Dentists Act, 1948, the Dentil) Council of India with the previous sanction of the Central Government hereby makes the following rules namely:— 1. Short title and commencement—(i) These rules may be called the, D&ntal Council of India Recruitment Rules, 2007. (ii) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. _________________ SCHEDULE______________ ______________ Name of post No. of post Scale ofpay Whether selection Whether the benefit of Age limit for post or non- added years of service direot recruits selection post admissible under rule 30 of die C.C.S. (Pen sion) Rules, 1972 1 2 3 4 5 6 Secretary 1 Rs. 10,000-325- Not Admissible. Less than 40 years 15,200 Applicable Educational and other Qualifications required for direct recruit > . S Whether age and educational qualification prescribed for direct recruits will apply in the case of promotees Period of probation, if any Method of Rectt.: Whether by direct rectt. or by promo tion or by deputation/ transfer and percen tage of the vacancies to be filled by various method 7 8 9 10 Educational Qualification: BDS Degree of a recognised University. Experience: A total of No 2 years Deputation (including short-term contract) failing which by direct recruitment 5 years in the profession of dentistry -- 12 of 21 -- A 12 THE OAZETTB OF INDIA: EXTRAORDINARY (Part HI—Sec. 4] Grade from which recruitment by promotion/ If n'DPC/Selection Committee exists, what is its composition dcputatton/transfcr/short-term contrnct/re- employmcnt is to.be made. 11 .12 Deputation (including short-term contract): From amongst Officers of the Central/State Government/ UnIversitles/PSUs/Autonomous Organizations and (i) holding analogous posts on regular basis, or (ii) with 5 years regular service in the scale of Rs. 8,000-13,500 and possessing the qualification prescribed for direct recruits In column 7, (The period of deputation including period of deputation in another ex-cadre post held immediately preceding this appointment in the same or some other Organization/Department of the Central Government shall ordinarily not exceed. three years. The Maximum age limit for appointment by deputation shall not be exceeding 56 years as on the closing date of receipt of applications.) (i) President —Chairman (ii) Vice President —Member (iii) Director (ME) —Member (iv) Two members to be nominated by the Executive Committee of . the Council. I 2 3 4 5 6 Deputy 1 Secretary 7,450-225,11,500 Not k Applicable Not Applicable Not Applicable 7 8 9 10 Not Applicable Not Applicable 2 years Composite Method Deputation (including short-term contract) Promotion. 11 12 . Deputation (including short-term contract)/Promo- (i) President —Chairman tion: From amongst Officers of the Central/State (ii) VicePresidcnt —Member Govemment/PSUs/Autonomous Bodies (i) Hold ing analogous- post on regular basis or with 2 years regular service in die scale of Rs. 6,500- 10,500 or equivalent; (li) Possessing graduate degree from recognized University; (iii) Having 2 years experience in handling Administrative • matters. Desirable: Knowledge of government rales/ regulations. The Departmental Assistant Secretary with 2 years of regular service will also he considered along with deputationists, and if selected the post will be deemed to have been filled by promotion. (iii) Director (ME) (iv) Two members to be nominated by the Executive Committee of the Council. —Member -- 13 of 21 -- [HPT IH—BPS 4] 13 u The departmental candidates will not be eligible for consideration for appointment by deputation. Similarly, the deputationist will not be considered for appointment by promotion. (The period of deputation including period of deputation in another ex-cadre post held immediately preceding this appointment in the same or some other Organization/Department of the Central Government shall ord inarily not exceed three years. The maximum age limit for appointment by deputation shall not be exceeding 56 years as on the closing date of receipt of applications). 2 3 4 5 6 Assistant I 1 Secretary I Rs. 6300-200-10300 Non- Selection Not.Applicable Not Applicable 7 8 9 10 Not Applicable Not Applicable Two years • Promotion failing which by Deputation (including short-term contract) 11 12 Promotion from the post of superintendent in the scale of550Q-175-9000 with 3 years regular service. Deputation ; From amongst Officers of the Centtal/State Govemment/PSUs/Autonomous Bodies (i) Holding analogous post on regular basis or with 3 years of regular service in the scale of Rs. 5,500—9,000 or equivalent; (ii) Possessing graduate degree from recognized University; (iii) Having 1 year experience in handling Administrative matters. Desirable: Knowledge of governments rules/ regulations. Secy./Asstt. Secretary (Admn.)—Member. The selection will be subject to tlie approval of President. DENTAL COUNCIL OF INDIA RECRUITMENT RULES Note: 1. Relaxations and concessions for Candidate belonging to Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, Other Back ward Class (OBC) and Physically handicapped provided by the Central Government &/or as amended from time to lime for appointment and promotion in the Council office shall be applicable. Other general instructions issued by the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pension, New Delhi for recruitment promotion to the SC/ST/OBC cadre shall befollowed. 2. The crucial date for determining the age limit shall be the closing date for the receipt of application. In case of recruitment made through the Employment Exchanges the crucial date for determining the age limit shall be last date up to which Employment Exchanges are asked to submit the names. 3. 'fhe qualification regarding experience is relaxable at the discretion of the competent authority in the case of candidates belonging to the Scheduled Castes/Scheduled Tribe, if at any stage of selection, the competent authority is of the opinion that the sufficient number of tlie candidates front these communities possessing the requisite experience are not likely to be available to fill up the vacancy reserved for them. For this purpose footnote may be added that appointing authority shall records the reasons for relaxing the qualifica tion regarding experience in writing while doing so. 4. Benefit of added years of service, admissible under Rule 30 of C.C.S. (Pension) Rules, 1972 as amended from time to time shall be applicable. 5. In the case posts except Group-A posts qualifications ate relaxable at tlie discretion of Appointing Authority in case the cand idates are found otherwise suitable for the job. 6. In case of promotions seniority-cum-merit criteria will be followed. Age & Educational qualifications shall be relaxable for die posts except Group-A Posts in the case of departmental candidates for promotion, if found otherwise suitable for die post. Qualifying service for selection posts may be relaxed by the competent authority upon the recommenda tions by the Departmental Promotion Committee. 7. Non-functional selection grade will be applicable after completing the qualifying service prescribed in the recruitment1 rules for respective higher promotion in the selection posts. 8. The assured career progression scheme as contained in O.M. No,35034/l/97-Estts. (D) dated 9-8-1999 issued by the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions (Department of Personnel and Training), New Delhi shall be applicable to die eriiployees of tlie Council. . Maj. Gen. (Retd.) Dr. P.N. AWASTHI, Secy.
